HEADING: MCP Server Setup
INTRO: FastComments exposes a stateless Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that lets AI coding assistants and agentic clients call the FastComments API directly. Each tool maps to an existing public API endpoint and is generated from the OpenAPI spec, so anything the REST API can do, an MCP client can do.
WHAT_IS_MCP_BODY: Model Context Protocol is an open standard for letting LLM clients (Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Cursor, and others) invoke server-side tools. The FastComments server runs over HTTP, requires no session state, and authenticates every request with your existing API key.
CHOOSE_KEY_HELP_PREFIX: The selected key is embedded in the generated URL and snippets. Manage keys on the
NO_KEYS_BODY: Create an API key before configuring an MCP client.
ENDPOINT_HELP: Use this URL anywhere an MCP client accepts a streamable HTTP endpoint.
CLAUDE_CODE_HELP: Run this from a terminal where the Claude Code CLI is installed.
CLAUDE_DESKTOP_HELP: Add this block to your client's MCP servers config (claude_desktop_config.json or Cursor's mcp.json).
SNIPPET_WARNING: Contains your API key. Do not share publicly.
